content.text:
I have a failure in playwright CI which doesn't happen locally, and it only fires in webkit:

Search tests
EnquiryForm › email flow
next »
completes full verification flow
info.spec.ts:79
9.5s
webkit
Error: expect(locator).toBeVisible() failed

Locator: getByText('Confirm your email')
Expected: visible
Timeout: 5000ms
Error: element(s) not found

Call log:
  - Expect "toBeVisible" with timeout 5000ms
  - waiting for getByText('Confirm your email')


  84 |             await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Send message' }).click();
  85 |
> 86 |             await expect(page.getByText('Confirm your email')).toBeVisible();
     |                                                                ^
  87 |
  88 |             const message = await waitForMessage(request, 'Confirm your enquiry');
  89 |             expect(message, 'Expected verification email in Mailpit').toBeTruthy();
    at /home/runner/work/next-www-toastboy/next-www-toastboy/e2e/info.spec.ts:86:64

Navigate to "/footy/info"— info.spec.ts:80

Fill "Verification Tester" getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Name' })— info.spec.ts:81

Fill "verify@example.com" getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Email' })— info.spec.ts:82
188ms
Fill "Please verify this enquiry." getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Message' })— info.spec.ts:83
82ms
Click getByRole('button', { name: 'Send message' })— info.spec.ts:84

Expect "toBeVisible" getByText('Confirm your email')— info.spec.ts:86

11.4s

screenshot
video
error-context
Let me look at the test and the form component to understand what's happening.
Let me check the notification auto-close config value.

The issue is a race condition in WebKit. After clicking "Send message", the component calls `notifications.show()` (loading state), then awaits the server action, then calls `notifications.update()` with `"Confirm your email"`. WebKit is apparently slower at rendering the notification update, or the server round-trip takes longer in CI on WebKit such that by the time the test polls at line 86, the notification either hasn't appeared yet or has already auto-closed.

The fix is to increase the `toBeVisible` timeout so the test waits long enough for the async flow to complete and the notification to appear, and optionally add a longer timeout on the notification itself for CI. The cleanest solution is to just increase the assertion timeout:

The root cause: the notification appears after the server action completes. The default Playwright timeout is 5000ms, but in WebKit on CI the server round-trip takes longer. Once the "loading" notification updates to "Confirm your email", it also starts a 4000ms auto-close timer — so the test must assert within that window. If the server is slow, the default 5s isn't enough to even see the notification appear.

The fix: increase the `toBeVisible` timeout on both assertions that check for the notification title.

input.old_string:
await expect(page.getByText('Confirm your email')).toBeVisible();
            await expect(page.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Name' })).toHaveValue('');
input.new_string:
await expect(page.getByText('Confirm your email')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 15000 });
            await expect(page.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Name' })).toHaveValue('');

result.output: The file /Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy/e2e/info.spec.ts has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.old_string:
await expect(page.getByText('Confirm your email')).toBeVisible();

            const message = await waitForMessage(request, 'Confirm your enquiry');
input.new_string:
await expect(page.getByText('Confirm your email')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 15000 });

            const message = await waitForMessage(request, 'Confirm your enquiry');

result.output: The file /Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy/e2e/info.spec.ts has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text: Both `toBeVisible` calls for "Confirm your email" now have a 15-second timeout. This gives WebKit enough time for the server action to complete and the notification to appear, while still leaving a buffer before the 4-second auto-close kicks in.
